title: pachycereus chrysomallus
intro: Pachycereus chrysomallus, also known as the 'Desert Candle', is a species of cactus that is native to the deserts of Baja California in Mexico. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Pachycereus chrysomallus include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide relief from respiratory problems. Herbal preparations of the plant include infusions, teas, and salves, which can be made by infusing the stems and flowers in hot water or oil.
